sprint: Clone Official Theme -- Scene Synthesis From Compiled Package Data
task: Answers a direct product request: let users clone an Official theme in World Builder to understand how it was built and use it as a real starting point, not just a technically-valid-but-empty 'No Scenes yet' clone. A compiled .vtheme is a confirmed one-way compile with no reverse path to the original authoring Scenes, so _materializeProjectFromPackage() now synthesizes one real, editable Scene per compiled Representation from what the package genuinely carries -- a Layout's Aspect Ratio becomes the Scene's canvas, a Representation's defaultFrame becomes its Holder's frame, Quote composition synthesizes zero Holders. Deliberately conservative: Layer Pack decorative content (captions, seals) is not reconstructed as editable Layers -- a disclosed gap, not a bug. representations/all.json is no longer written for a materialized project (validator.js only hard-requires layouts/frames/layer-packs; scenes/ alone satisfies the representations OR-rule), preventing a real would-be duplicate-Representation bug a naive implementation would have shipped. Verified via a direct Validation check against the real on-disk Museum Gallery package, a real headless Build+Publish confirming exactly 3 non-duplicate Representations, and the real Workspace UI showing 3 correctly-named synthesized Scenes. Deep Clone Fidelity's prior strict byte-identical check for layouts/representations is knowingly relaxed to a semantic-equivalence check for this one change; every other field stays strictly byte-identical. Full regression across 14 scripts + goldenBuild.js unchanged. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
